channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Oil price — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil price

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-03-02 18:57 UTC

OP

>Street of Hormuz de-facto closed for at 24h
>20-25% of global oil supply cut from market
>Refineries attacked and closed in Saudi Arabia
>Tankers attacked on/near Street of Hormuz
>Tanker insurance fees through the roof
>Regime wants no negotiations with US
>Army and Guards still loyal to Regime
>Bloomberg predicts prices around 100$/barrel Brent

Still Oil price is going down. Merely up 5% from Friday. What the fuck is going on?!

Early replies (first 3)

61925169
Priced in

61925191
>>61925117
>>Regime wants no negotiations with US
Nice try moshi

61925221
>>61925169
How can an indefinite cut of 20-25% of global oil supply be priced in with like 16$ increase since start of 2026?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61925235
>>61925117
>regime says
What regime?

61925241
>>61925117
>War was being partially priced in already over the last weeks
>OPEC announced production increases
>As US is not putting boots on the ground, nobody thinks this war can last long
>Oil price isn't as inelastic anymore as it used to be. Stop living in the past
>There was a glut of oil already before this. It is providing a buffer for some time

61925300
>>61925117
I swear biz posters are stupid lol
>20% of the supply got cut off.
>Fridays open was $65
>Mondays (today) open was $75
65/75=86.6
14% increase.

61925401
>>61925300
Brent closed 73.20 and is currently at 77.40.
How is 4.20 a 20% increase?
Even the opening at 81.57 is only 11%.
Also the demand is very inelastic.

61925417
I am watching CNBC. They are downplaying the severity of everything. They seem very desperate to push this narrative. I could be wrong but it seems like cope. Everyone is going with the "nothing ever happens" narrative. I think the very real risks are being downplayed.

61925431
>>61925191
They literally just said that, it’s on the news.

61925438
>>61925241
>OPEC announced production increases
Is that the same OPEC that's getting blown the fuck up right now. I'm not sure it's up to them

61925441
>>61925241
>not putting boots on the ground
The also just said on the news that this is likely to happen now.

61925451
I am reading /biz/. They are upplaying the severity of everything. They seem very desperate to push this narrative. I could be wrong but it seems like cope. Everyone is going with the "happening" narrative. I think the very fake and gay risks are being upplayed.

61925461
>>61925401
Brent closed at 73 and just went to 80
Which is a 10% change.
the open was a 5% gap up
And then the market went up another 5% in 4 hours.

that's a 10% move up in FOUR hours. I swear the people here know nothing...

61925462
>>61925451
Then there's no risk or opportunity here meaning there's nothing to post about.

61925465
>>61925417
If China isn't retarded they'll stuff Iran full of free weapons and Iran blast those weapons all over the region. Like US can attack Russia via Ukraine, China can now attack US interests via Iran. It's a very comfy situation, if they're not retarded they won't let the war end

61925478
>>61925117
The toilet is mid clog sir.

61925481
>>61925417
I always wondered why no one is talking about the blown up tankers in the straight. Seems like cover up is real. No way US or any other military can defend the straight against Iran. On single drone-droppe handgrenade is enough to blow up an LNG Tanker. This thing is not over for at least a week. That means that 140m Barrels are taken out of circulation. The shit hits the fan when the first strategic oil reserves are empty

61925482
>>61925465
I suspect they're retarded. I mean it's kinda late for that. They could have saw this war coming and used Iran as their proxy. For both China and Russia this should come as a complete shock. The Venezuela situation as well, what did China do about that? This sends a pretty clear message to the rest of the world that China isn't a serious ally. They're just who you go to when nobody else wants to be your friend. If they were going to take Taiwan they could have had the US tied up in a total mess in the Middle East while they did it. But that's not what is happening.

Even if Iran doesn't hold for very long they could still seriously disrupt the energy market. That's what I'm worried about now.

61925495
>>61925117
just go long retard

61925507
>>61925221
The US, and by extension the West, control the overwhelming majority of the world's ready supply. Venezuela is no longer fighting the West or flaunting its rules, and Russia is currently falling on its sword and dying, so the only independent suppliers are the Iran/Iraq area, which is partially mitigated by the Saudis and UAE being West-aligned, and Indonesia.

In short, its priced in because fuck you, that's why. Forgetting that America is the World Protagonist is on you, not us.

61925522
How do you guys see this war lasting more than 2 months?
US doesn't have that many missiles to spare.
US won't start an invasion. That would be wildly unpopular, Trump is already one of the least supported US president in history and he needs for there not to be a Democrat sweep in the midterm elections.
US can't afford a full scale war right now.

61925534
>>61925522
>US won't start an invasion. That would be wildly unpopular
>Trump is already one of the least supported US president in history
Based on that logic there should be no war in Iran. The Republican party is neocon driven. The neocons are in control. Trump's inner circle are all neocons. They don't care about anything except getting their war. They got it. They are happy. Fuck the midterms as far as they are concerned.

61925550
>>61925534
A quick clean victory is what he wanted as that would have likely boosted his ratings

61925567
>>61925550
That's what they say every time. Every fucking time. I heard what was coming out of Pete Hegseth's mouth earlier. This isn't going to be a forever war. This isn't going to be another Iraq. But Trump says he's not afraid to "go long", he says "boots on the ground" are an option. The message he is sending is that it's going to take as long as it's going to take. These people are neocons. They sell the war on the premise that it will be easy to win. If it isn't they don't back down.

61925605
>>61925417
>I think the very real risks are being downplayed
Agreed. "Nothing ever happens" has become a crowded trade.

61925615
>>61925482
>kinda late for that
What do you mean late, it's been happening since day 1 of this war. Iran bombed a ton of NATO bases, you think China isn't interested in that? The only question is whether or not China is fully committed to this. They could be bombing those bases for years, forcing NATO to move more and more weapons to the middle of the desert so that they can shoot a 10k drone with a 10mil missile.
And China did supply Iran with a bunch of stuff between these two wars, let's see if they continue, they're trying to be sneaky. Not sure why though, EU isn't sneaky about giving weapons to Ukraine.
>>61925522
>he thinks US decides when it ends
If US doesn't do a land invasion, Iran can just keep bombing those NATO bases forever. Why stop

61925652
>>61925615
Well you had the supreme leader sitting in his house instead of hiding in a bunker when he had about a week's notice that the US was coming to fuck his shit right up. Now you have a situation where they're scrambling to rebuild their leadership. So how is Iran going to be a good proxy without leadership and organization?

61925667
>>61925117
>oil is only up 5%
scroll out retard oil is going up since december

61925671
>>61925117
priced in

61925673
>>61925300
He's talking about the oil that passes the Strait of Hormuz, not the current price action tard.

61925704
>>61925673
I'm fully aware of what he is talking about.
Read his post again:
>Still Oil price is going down. Merely up 5% from Friday. What the fuck is going on?!

61925726
>>61925704
You're right

61925729
>>61925481
BBC News is now reporting that ships have been attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. Cat is out of the bag now.

item.title: Dominos are falling — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Dominos are falling

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-03-02 18:55 UTC

OP

Iran is blowing up oil refineries in the UAE as we speak.

Early replies (first 3)

61923034
Oil price go up: I stack.
Oil price go down: I stack.
AMA.

61923035
Two words

61923036
>>61923033
>IQ 276
Wow, I have to follow his trades, NOWW!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61923047
>>61923033
This person does not know what rate hikes are lmao. The FED does not hike rates due to inflation from commodity supply issues. Rate hikes are used to cool an overheated economy, in this case the economy is already slowed plenty and hiking rates is the exact opposite of what's in the playbook right now.

61923050
>>61923047
FED doesn't cut into an oil shock either

61923058
>>61923047
it's literally not a real person, it's an AI bot account that is using an AI likeness of Younghoon Kim, the world record IQ holder, to shill XRP and other bullshit

61923070
Sum Dum Fook has spoken

61923076
Biggest oil refinery in the world hit in the UAE(Aramco). Flames go up, price go up.

61923081
>>61923076

61923083
>>61923070
I wonder if he's related to Sum Ting Wong?

61923090
>>61923033
Nothing ever happens

61923101
>>61923033
>step 10
please god i have 40k usdc on hyperliquid ready to buy, that would be 2btc

61923110
>>61923076
>llOW DOBN

61923124
>>61923034
Stack what?

61923127
>>61923033
A flash crash just flew over my house!

61923129
>>61923076
>>61923081

61923181
>>61923033
I'm not investing in oil, too many other countries which can scale up production to compensate for these losses.

61923256
>>61923124
that's a codeword for he lets black men lay ontop of his wife

61923261
>>61923033
Easiest scam ever to pull off on the internet is put "IQ: 375" and theres a subsection of ultra retards who will become cult followers

61923304
>>61923033
why would the FED do rate hikes this is stupid

61923316
>>61923304
To make the situation worse obviously

61923344
More like 27.6

61923395
>>61923033
xrp to 20$
fucking china

61923417
>>61923256

61923431
>>61923033
Kek I bet big on emergency rate hike on kalshi

61923541
>>61923033
By end of next week? Lets me calculate are these two weeks?

61923792
>>61923033
>the guy who bought the top of xrp from literal uber drivers has a financial outlook
lmao

61923812
>>61923033
>Step 6: Emergency rate hikes

61923831
>>61923058
>it's an AI bot account that is using an AI likeness of Younghoon Kim, the world record IQ holder, to shill XRP and other bullshit
That's fucking hilarious

61923868
>>61923033
>IQ276
That's better than Isaac Newton. i gotta follow him, you just know his plays are gonna make bank

61924876
>>61923036
Was just thinking anyone so insecure they lead with their IQ is clearly a self-important midwit.

61925114
>>61923033
>Full market capitulation
Follow by The Great Taking.

61925725
These are wet dreams of a wild cunt. if you trust him, see picrel

item.title: Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-02 18:51 UTC

OP

>Have an easy office job
>Mostly just scroll on my phone
>Literally just do barely anything and make salary
>Realize I could make money in my phone during this time
>Start making AI art, get up to $100 a month from subs
Anyone aware of something that could make more than this and is primarily done on your phone. I do not mind doing it on my PC at home too, as I already do this with AI stuff For now, AI is easy enough and extra money is extra money but more cash flows will always be better
Note, I want actual income, not “Just sell options on your phone while at your job bro”

Early replies (first 3)

61924922
So basically asian tits make me straight haha

61924932
I'm considering getting a 3d printer farm plus I know how to work with the files and make custom things but idk seems effort

61924936
>>61924922
I’m happy for you anon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924994
Just get another WFH job.

61925190
>>61924882
what AI art?

61925207
My passive income is having $700k in VFV and collecting dividends.

61925508
>>61925190
Anime girls with big breasts
>>61925207
Well I am also investing but only have for a year hence why I wanna get more cash asap for compounding

61925560
>>61924882
how much money do I need to have to make a woman like that fall in love with me. like actual true love.

61925564
>>61925560
True love requires no money

61925575
>>61925564
Nah it requires at least stability

61925594
>>61925207
>Just have 700k
I calculated my breakoff point where I can live entirely off safety nets indefinitely is 865k. The good thing is at least I'm getting closer

61925611
>>61925594
High seven figure hell is real. I want to quit working but Canada is stupid expensive to live in. Probably gonna fuck off to the Caribbeans once I hit $1 million USD

61925619
>>61924922
You're 35. It's time to grow up and have a wife AND kids.

61925623
>>61925611
Oops meant high six figure.

61925624
>>61925611
Well the numbers are based on how much you can extract monthly while still living like a human and not like a nigger so yeah

61925650
I was considering candle making but it's saturated by women

61925663
Hey man, I know it's difficult, but listen, the honest truth is that most phone based side hustles that feel easy are capped low and crowded, so your hundred a month from AI art is actually normal, not underperformance, and the uncomfortable reality is that meaningful extra income usually comes from either building an audience, selling something people repeatedly buy, or doing work others are too lazy or too skilled to do, which means if you want to beat that number from your phone you should focus on things like running niche subscription pages with consistent content drops, flipping underpriced items locally through marketplace apps, managing short form content pages that you later monetize with affiliates, or doing simple but in demand freelance tasks through mobile friendly platforms, because the people who quietly make real side money are not chasing magic passive tricks, they are stacking small repeatable systems and letting them compound while everyone else keeps searching for effortless cash.

61925701
>>61925560
I’m a doctor and found a woman similiar to that but white. I already left her tho because i want to fuck differebt kinds of hotties. So whatevet doctor makes + social status.

61925705
>>61924882
i fucking NEED a gf this hot
how do i do it ? money? looksmaxing?
please someone tell me how

were off to a great start

61912801

61914222

>not 400x400

Regardless, I appreciate the thread and the effort. Weirdly, I was thinking of EFG just the other day.

61914231

61914300

61915467

It was low bandwidth versions of popular memes.

61915797
>>61914300
Same but make it 2004 and few months of break once in a while

61917312
>>61906458
>>61907301
I would really love to go back to post dial up but pre smartphone internet world and just stay there, basically everything past that point has been a downgrade and the few things that have improved aren't worth it at all

61917331
The saddest thing is that as you age and age you realize that the new gens will say the same thing. Their real quality is what they know too.

61917585
>incorrect file names
Salvia officinalis in fields

61917849
>>61906370
>jpg

61918416
great thread. i miss /b/.

61918426
This was not spam.

61918486
>>61909212
Anxiety? What? There’s nothing scary about that photo

61918948
>>61906370
Good thread. It's been a while since OP wasn't a faggot

61918968
>>61918416

It’s a cesspit now. Dont even look. Man I’m 46! Where did the time go.

61920110
>>61918968
dang unc, go outside

61920123
Great thread, man.

61920179
>>61906449
>oopsie
winrar

61920392
>>61906370
efg go up. ascend into higher realms of meme existence

61920744
>>61907301
if the world just ended in 2016 we'd be a lot better off
nothing has been good since then

61921246
>>61906387
Kek'd irl

>>61906426
>>61906431
LOL

61921344
>>61906458
It still is. Ever seen that image of the Chinese kid with a boot on his head, then the next image shows that his arm was in the boot? That's you. You can take the boot off your head and laugh at anytime.

61924708
>>61906449
kys

61924733

61924820

61925046

61925061

item.title: Anyone else getting the itch? — 73 replies — 25.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else getting the itch?

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-03-02 16:13 UTC

OP

The big boys'll go back up, that's guaranteed money. But some others will 10x, 100x, 1000x. I'm feelin' like it's SHIB's turn.

Early replies (first 3)

61900851
>>61899833
Checked

61900872
>>61899833
I'll just keep buying small amounts of it for the rest of my life, and see what happens.

61900948
>>61899833
i believe in shib

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61912911
this thread smells like india
>YES SAAAR BUY TOAD SAAR IT IS NEW DOGE SAAR

61912963
>>61912911
It’s the next shib not doge, retard

61912969
I boughted more Shib today. It ain't easy being a good boy in these doggy-dog times.

61913401
>>61912969
I never thought it would be this low again. I shlopped another 50m today too.

61913435
>>61913401
Could’ve got close to 1T toad for that price

61915673
>>61912963
Man... can you imagine the seethe on this board if this turns out to be true.

61917994
>>61913435
I made that pic kek

61917997
I had to sell all my toad though

61918166
>>61917997
What if it does what it’s intended to do and you sold? I’m not sure I could live with myself. I’d definitely rather lose the money than live with that type of fuckup for the rest of my life

61918170
Don't make last cycles mistake anon
Stay away from memecoins and bitcoin, 100% in ETH or XMR

61919529
Shib is fucking dead

61919535
>>61919529
yeah, kinda is
Thankfully i still got in early enough that there was never any chance of not making a profit despite missing out on the big gains through greed but there's nothing but a slow bleed right now and i've seen nothing that means it might pump again

61921116
Bro imagine buying that

61921137
Not today

61921143
No such thing as a free lunch

61921144
>>61899833
none of these shitcoins are gonna do big movements again retard

61921169
>>61921144
No such thing as a free lunch

61921558
Just bought some more boys, let's go

61921644
>>61901257
This already rugged.

61921961
>>61921558
Ummm based?

61921988
>>61921644
Checked and true

61922004
kek turd killer still being shilled here in 2026
lmfao nice AI pics btw

61922014
>>61922004

61922070
>>61922004
You have 2 more weeks wagie

61922078
I hope you don’t buy toad

61922734
No one is buying toad in a shib thread! Shib is going to 14x to ath and toad will go to zero. Shib already has an etf coming and toad has desperate curry flavored shills. LMAO!

61923250
>>61922734
When toad moons YOU'RE going to seethe, when toad moons YOU'RE going to rope!

61923260
>>61899833
I am in anon, people are too stupid to skip this one.
And some Doge in case the elom makes a comeback.

61924787

item.title: ""THE FUTURE OF FINANCE"" — 31 replies — 25.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
""THE FUTURE OF FINANCE""

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-02 17:51 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61921606
>>61921599
>stablecoins
>exchanges
Deserved.

61921612
>>61921599
What is slippage and who would set it to 99%.... Probably above 100 iq bigbrain bros fr fr no cap!

61921797
>>61921599
why did you repost this shit from the last decade?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61922103
That’s why I use Rubic unironically. It’s the only swapping service that has decent privacy features.

61922206
>>61921599
Proof of stake was a retarded concept in hindsight

61922296
>>61921599
should have used cow swap or curve or whatever
>>61922103
rubic's website, like chainlink, stopped working with brave browser integration.
rubic is a scam anyway

61922350
https://www.lightspark.com/glossary/stablecoin-sandwich
thanks for this op, one less way i can get rekt from now on

61922425
>>61921599
once you add all the losses from smart contract exploits and MEV bot shenanigans it becomes quickly apparent that only a fucking moron would expect institutions to adopt eth as a transaction layer.

61923392
>>61922425
What is slippage?

61923405
>>61921599
Guess he should have set a higher gas fee lmao

61923519
>>61922206
>PoS
it's in the name

61923521
>>61921797
it’s last week you retard

61923564
>>61922425
yep, obviously not when they can’t control it.

61923565
Meanwhile.......

61923572
>>61922425
link solves this unironically

61923784
>>61921599
Why isn't SVR used everywhere?

61923804
>>61921599
>eth
There’s your problem.

61924939
>>61921599
Wtf does this even mean in plain English? I'm somewhat retarded, can someone explain

61924989
>>61921599
Wait a second that's not a transaction that's random AI slop

61925063
>>61924989
You're telling me you don't use "Uniowap V3"?

61925067
>>61924939
https://www.coinbase.com/learn/crypto-glossary/what-are-sandwich-attacks-in-crypto
only happens with PoS shitcoins like ETH

61925211
I'll try to explain it as simply as possible:

Everything in a blockchain is public and these swaps (like uniswap) use pools of tokens to facilitate trades...so a USDT/USDC swap has an equal amount of each token.

As people trade more USDT for USDC, the price of USDC goes up relative to USDT...so basically as the number of USDC goes to 0, the price of USDC (in terms of USDT) goes to infinity. The idea is you always want an even amount in each pool to keep prices stable.

People run blockchain validators with software called "flashbots" which is very quick software. These validators get to decide the order of transactions in a blockchain, and they generally order transactions based on who is willing to pay the most in txn fees.

So an analogy would be something like: you walk into a currency exchange and say "I'M GONNA SWAP $215K (i.e. USDC) for Swiss Francs (i.e. USDT)! Some guy hears you say this and cuts the line to get ahead of you (i.e. outbids your txn so he can do an exchange before you).

He basically then runs up to the counter and buys a bunch of Swiss Francs before your turn comes up.

Because he just bought a large amount, the exchange booth now has fewer Swiss Francs left and a lot more dollars. So the exchange rate changes...Swiss Francs just became more expensive.

Now it’s your turn.

You still go through with your $215K exchange, but the rate is no longer the fair 1:1 rate you expected. Since the booth’s supply of Swiss Francs is now low, the clerk gives you a terrible rate. You get way fewer francs than you should have.

Right after your trade finishes, the guy who cut in line steps back up to the counter.

Now the booth has tons of dollars (because of your big swap) and Swiss Francs are overpriced. So he sells the Swiss Francs he just bought back to the booth at this new, better rate.

He walks away with more dollars than he started with.

61925234
>>61924989
We are currently witnessing the beginning of the end, not long before the internet will become unusable

61925244
>>61921606
does that include the nasdaq?

61925264
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=UnPIVmkfSbs
>is this guy talking about metalicus?

61925270
>>61925211
thanks chatgpt

61925297
>>61921599
I interviewed with a company that does this.
Gravity Team.
This is exactly what they do. They have bots operating on 60 exchanges jewing everyone.
Owned by a Russian jew.

61925316
>>61925270
Curious...why do you think that response was generated by AI? It is sad that any time you try to give an explanation on biz now, people just assume it is GPT. The rate at which people are offloading all thinking to AI is pretty demoralizing actually.

The original response to that post would have been:

>go back
>reddit spacing

or something similarly gay

61925340
>>61925297
This isn't really "jewing" people...it is a feature of how these networks work and there are plenty of ways to avoid being sandwich attacked like this. So if you get sandwich attacked this badly, it's just ignorance. There are also networks that have fair ordering natively.

61925343
>>61921606
>>exchanges
It's not an exchange

61925444
>>61921599
>sandwich attack
https://youtu.be/4TEbuSw_QBM

item.title: Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l… — 34 replies — 14.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-03-02 16:43 UTC

OP

Hyperliquid

>decentralized perp and spot exchange
>trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war
>genius level founders. Harvard, MIT and caltech.
>no vc funding from day 1
>31% geneis airdop tokens to users minting millionaires for early usrs.
>97% all platfrom fees spent and buy HYPE tokens from open market and sent to burned wallet.


Holding HYPE is comfy AF

Early replies (first 3)

61918612
>>61918597
Buy an ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES

61918623
>>61918597
>Shilled by literally all of Twitter fags including scammers like Arthur
>Token not needed
Yeah, no.

61918628
>>61918623
why don't people say "stock not needed". its a crypto company and the coin is basically the stock.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61918707
It probably has the best price action out there. And it's not listed on most major exchanges

>>61918695
Isn't hype used as gas for the hyperliquid chain?

61918832
>>61918623
hype is great. non kyc leverage trading??? plus it had a multitude of apps being built to take advantage of it from fully automated leverage trading apps like jest to shit like dreamcash. Claude being used on it too. its here to stay

61918837
>>61918832
More info about those apps? I thought it was only used for the hyperliquid exchange

61918843
>>61918597
Looks like asters butt plug.

61918925
>>61918597
shit volume on commodities
have fun getting adled
dot hl are insufferable fags

61919310
>>61918612
HYPEBTC has a nice bullflag setup

61919323
>>61918623
>token not needed

All 97% of all fees generated by the Hyperliquid exchange is used to buy HYPE tokens and gets sent to burn address.
$1.3 Billion is fees so far. All on chain.

0xfefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefefe

61919354
>>61919323
Is the hype token used as gas for the chain?

61919376
Fuck hyperliquid, they forced cz the good guy to giganuke the market (like he did in 2022 to destroy ftx/sbf), but these fuckers are still alive.
They need to disappear so cz can allow the market to recover.

61919383
>>61919376
I don't hold any hype but I use the exchange on a daily basis and it works better than even centralized exchanges. It's the best product out there.

61919391
>>61919354
Yes. Hype is used as gas on the HyperEVM chain.

There are two chains in Hyperliquid ecosystem.

HyperCore - trading engine. No gas needed. Pay with trading fees which is used to buy and burn HYPE.

HyperEVM - smart-contract layer. HYPE used as bas.

61920112
I was thinking about grabbing a bag in late Jan and then it pumped again. Serves me right for wanting to buy this under 20 burgerbux

61920124
>>61920112
Same, bro. I just hold btc and trade on hyperliquid. I wanted to get myself a good back of hype, it's the best project out there and the team are not niggers. They want hype to go up. This weekends pump is because everybody is using it to trade crypto and commodities.

One of the few chains that is actually used. No marketing, no bullshit kantian philosophy or any kind of german idealism talk like Sergey or Charles hoskinson

61920169
How do I even buy Hype in the US? I need to use a VPN?

61920469
>>61919376

61920537
can i trade spot btc on there?

61920572
>>61920537
yes. Crypto, stocks, commodities... both spot and perps

61920574
Forex too

61921651
>>61920169
it's on Coinbase now starting Feb 2026. Not sure if it's available in USA.

VPN is very useful. expressvpn

61921686
Funny , this thread popped up because hype's about the moon

61921966
>>61918612
tell that to bloomberg you stupid nigger

61921981
>>61918623
>hurr im not going to buy this multi-billion marketcap token because [insert random person i dont like] is in it!!!
You are thinking like a pleb here and will simply miss out on profits this way.

61922017
>>61918597
I only have 250 hype and refuse to buy up here. It has to retrace back to the low 20's at some point.

Also shilling hype here is a waste. This entire board is so underwater on their bags they're in the mariana trenches.
>Link generals
>Avi generals
>BBBY/GME

I said this to myself earlier this year but Jeff and Vitalik are truly the last 2 people in this entire fucking space that still hasn't soul their souls yet

61922034
>>61921981
Look up GMX.

61924490
Sigh
These poor souls

61924506
edgex is better

61924534
>>61921981
why would he want to buy something that is already multibillions?

61924803
>>61918597
>sent to burned wallet.

why are you lying? the tokens arent burned, they are kept in their treasury

they probably will never have a need to touch those tokens, but they arent burned

61924887
>>61918707
So, the gas is, on a direct level paid in USDC. This is why you one-time sign, and can trade without having to sign each time, unlike GMX. Your trading fees are gas.
These trading fees are used to buy & burn hype. That's why the exchange is a fee collecting engine, and purchasing a share in said engine is by buying hype, which also serves as governance.

Also /biz/ is retarded these days, one-shotted by chainlink and not knowing about anything outside of the cult bubble.

61924972
>>61918597
>source, closed

item.title: Buy alts now. Anything with fully diluted supply and reasonable history will suffice. It's quite clear at this point alt… — 16 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Buy alts now. Anything with fully diluted supply and reasonable history will suffice. It's quite clear at this point alt…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-02 17:02 UTC

OP

Buy alts now. Anything with fully diluted supply and reasonable history will suffice.

It's quite clear at this point alts are doing an XRP of last run. Most have had their relatively shallow ~85% drawdown at this point and will continue to range from here with small pumps inbetween. BTC might drop to finish it's bottom later this year but alts will likely pump before that happens to respect the Feb26 bottom. You're looking at a mild spring/summer pump. The real pump won't be until late 2027/early 2028 (frontrun again, yes). Alts will most likely just follow a gradual path until that point with no real noteable price action so if you buy now you'll need to be patient.

Fud is extremely high and nobody is buying alts. Next run is likely to be alt centric.

Early replies (first 3)

61923244
>>61923233
$1 doge?

61923248
>>61923233
ive been on this godforsaken board for nearly 10y now, and on average, 1 out of 120 predictions seem to be close to 80%. so im gonna go out on a limb and call bs because you're just hopeposting for your own peace of mind, like most do

61923254
>>61923248
Then you should know when /biz/ is screaming alts are dead and it's the end of the world you should be buying (now).

All bottom indiciators are signaling I can't help you any more than that.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61923404
>>61923254
you're not helping anyone. Imo the most true prediction is always the one that causes the most pain. I'm gonna assume we get a bulltrap with the clarity act to fuck over everyone and then total capitulation by end of summer, and then we get rigged green candles

61923412
>>61923254
>>61923404
like, they frontran the cycle, making marketsentiment think this is it, no more cycles just upwards. Then FUCKED everyone. Then we got the small pump in end of 2025, fucking over everyone again that went in there thinking NOW its the time. Now, they crashed the market and everyone on le reddit and 4chan perpetuated that, oh, actually the cycles are still perfect, complete with an anon posting a year back and calling the top based on cycles.
Now the whole market thinks we're in bearmarket with a glimmer of hope (clarity act), so when that delivers, and only gives a small pump, then imo we're at absolute demoralization. Because the only people bagholding right now are the ones that think Clarity Act is gonna do something, which are the utility and schizo coins (stellar, cardano, XRP, link)

61923443
>>61923412
not buying your underwater shitcoin bags, sorry

61923453
>>61923443
kek ESL

61923457
>>61923233
>>61923244
>>61923248
i want to buy XRP or XLM, but im afraid the price is gonna drop so far that I won't know the best time to buy

there will be a rebound when people start hating bitcoin for all the reasons they should have over a decade ago

61923459
>>61923453
frega un cazzo del tuo inglese di merda coglione ebreo

61923496
>doing an XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

61923506
>>61923412
You've just repeated what i've said though. Yes there'll likely be a bull trap this year but it will still offer much higher prices that now. Not that we won't return to similar prices by the end of the year but I just don't think alts set new lows. Bitcoin, probably.

>>61923457
XRP is imo a risky buy as we've no precedent how it might perform from now on if what i've said plays out. I can see 10-12 usd in the next run.

61923509
Alts are actually going to 0. Don't buy alts
>t. oldfag that has seen this rodeo before

61923525
theres zero reason to believe alts will go up again ever. bitcoin went from 15k to 125k and they barely moved, the only notable one was xrp

its very obvious the risk vs reward just isnt there for alts anymore. any of them can fall another 90% and at best you're looking at a 3-5x if you enter the exact bottom

61923536
alts went up because it was the best casino. prediction markets sports betting are better casinos now. binary yes/no outcomes are much easier to stomach than the never ending list of trash that makes alts go to zero

61923781
>>61923254
Truth. I'll rely on greed to return.

61925137
>>61923525
>theres zero reason to believe alts will go up again ever
Exactly the mentality i'm looking for to mark bottoms. Buying bottoms is never easy and it always feels like you're being stupid buying at bottoms.

item.title: Was telling some mechanic wfh engineer how he has no life because he has a job. — 38 replies — 7.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Was telling some mechanic wfh engineer how he has no life because he has a job.

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-03-02 14:07 UTC

OP

Was telling some mechanic wfh engineer how he has no life because he has a job. He replies with
>if I had no life, I would kms
I go
>you spend most of your time at work. 40 hours a week and 5 out of 7 days. Work IS your life.
He goes
>no, I sleep 8 hours, work 7.5 hours and chill 8 hours.

Are normies this retarded?

Early replies (first 3)

61918491
he's out there living life right now on a Saturday night while you're here posting

61918517
>>61918491
I don’t need to do anything on any specific day like a working class person.

61918538
By default, because he doesn't post on this site, that makes him more free and an overall better person than you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61918637
>>61918478
You sound like a pathetic retarded faggot trying to justify his meaningless life.

61918676
>>61918607
No it isn’t. Prices drop but people lose their jobs.

61918684
Why are you here if you are wealthy?

61918702
>>61918684
Epstein posted here bro

61918710
>>61918605
Average home price in my city is 900k.
And isn’t the median household income 60k?

61918874
>>61918605
>>61918605
They wouldn't give me a loan because I don't make enough money, so I just bought my $525,000 house outright with my savings. Fuck those bank cocksuckers. Now I just have to pay property taxes.

61920119
>>61918676
If you haven't saved up since 2008 waiting for the REAL housing crash, you are simply NOT going to make it.

as said, skill issue.

61920132
Your ancestors worked twelve hour days, six days a week doing subsistence labor for potatoes, eating meat only during major holidays but you think working eight hours a day in an air-conditioned office and purchasing an international vacation with your labor hours is some kind of slavery because you watched Fight Club. A lot of you are on the border of delusion with the nonsense you spout. You have no knowledge of the past, zero conceptualization of the future. But here you are, making half-assed posts on a horticulture enthusiast image forum.

61920148
>>61920132
Youre right the ancestors were kind of dumb

61920162
>>61920132
No they fucking didn’t.
Have you ever grown a potato in your life? You work hard a few days to get it all in the ground then you simply wait and chill until harvest. Fucking noble retard. Can’t even till.

61920163
>>61920119
wait wait wait, are you saying you gonna spend 18 years of saving up the liquidity from waging and risk taking to dump it onto a depreciating asset that has a naturally declining demand AND is going to get nuked because it has been used as a same asset re-leveraged ponzi collateral for 60 years?
AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

61920166
>>61920119
>he "saves" up before making a purchase
ishiggidiggi

61920175
>>61920162
its really about time to cull the pseudo aristocracy of ponzi players of polity, bureaucracy, banker communists, subsidized "privates" and 50+ — they all deserve to die unnatural deaths

61920178
>>61920162
You’re legitimately dumb if you think our ancestors just sat around doing nothing. They worked their dicks off for shelter and food, with not much to spare.

61920183
>>61920178
>its history
>its culture
>do as I say
>please dont kill me
too late. You gonna die

61920187
>>61920178
Yeah, I'm with this guy. Subsistence farming fucking sucked, hence why thurdies would rather toil away in a mine inhaling sulfur fumes and dunking their nuts in mercury, rather than work on a field as a farmer

61920201
>>61920187
Its not that bad it just pays pretty bad. I think.

61920206
>>61920183
No idea what you’re talking about. You’re delusional if you think labor conditions and incentives for work haven’t exponentially gotten better over the past century.
>>61920187
People act like farming is some dope shit. Actually no, it sucks balls which is why we get immigrants to do it.

61920210
>>61920201
If you’re a laborer, yeah. If you’re a farmer with several hundred acres, you make an okay living. It’s a weird sector because the government can’t let you go out of business, so you’ll see white trash in a brand new truck they bought with farm bill money.

61920218
>>61920210
Many farmers went out of business recently lol

61920226
>>61920119
You are retarded.

61920228
>>61918478
lmao, I barely do any work and bought a second house on a lake last year, how poor are you all that you can't even afford one house?

61920234
>>61920218
This usually happens when the third or fourth generation realizes it’s a terrible job. Nobody is taking over for a relative, it just sucks. So they’ll sell the land for several million to some real estate developer probably.

61920258
>>61920163
>>61920166
>>61920226
real estate kikes are scared shitless

61920261
>>61920206
>back in my days
Gramps, your life is over. Your perception of reality is as relevant as a wet fart from last week. Honest suggestion, instead of trying to shill your ideals, kill yourself

61920279
>>61920258
they certainly are. The only reason the akiya market hasnt yet corrected to 1920 levels is because the greatest fools: funds, banks, asset management companies and other ponzi players have acquired the boomer stock that was thrown on the market over the last 4 years with the sole purpose to not see the collateral value of their re-leveraged ponzi schemes melt away and leave them as their creditors bankrupt, holding worthless concrete wood boomer boxes

61920336
>>61920132
expect they were drunk the whole time or on drugs.
and those Drugs and booze used to be cheap!
now you need to be a highly functionally addict with a full time job to afford the habits

61923763
>>61920132
the former is generative and natural surrounded by family. the latter is deracinated ZOG shit surrounded by freaks on ssris and birth control
also fully 1/3 the year was holidays when you look at tudor period.

my ancestors caved in the skulls of the largest animals on earth and ate nothing but their flesh for their entire lives with nothing but pointy sticks and some clever strategy
when they weren't creating tools and buildings to live in, they were relaxing after engorging themselves on these animals, resting and celebrating before the next glorious hunt
your "history" you reference is the short time between 13k years ago and current, the agricultural revoltuin, AKA when slavery allowed for the domestication and slaveries of massive human cattle farms. You glorify this because you have slaves blood, slave mentality, and a slave's soul.
